reagents.name: Calcined Vitriol (Oxford MS)
reagents.description: A sharp, white salt, purified by the heat of the London sun.

variant_options.name: Calcined Vitriol (Oxford MS)
variant_options.description: A sharp, white salt, purified by the heat of the London sun.

variant_options.name: Green Vitriol (Paris MS)
variant_options.description: A crude, emerald-green earth, favored by the skeptics of the Paris circle.

reagents.name: Antimony
reagents.description: The semi-metal for the Glass of Antimony.

reagents.name: Philosopher's Lead
reagents.description: Lead that has received the influence of vitriolic spirit.

commodities.description: High-value raw silk, often traded from the interior at Scanderoon. Essential for European luxury markets.
A dense, stable metal used in the initial stages of the Great Work. Highly valued in Algiers for maritime ballistics.
A staple of the Mediterranean trade. High nutritional value with significant markup in London markets.
The liquid soul of metals. Essential for the creation of the Red Tincture, but extremely volatile during storage.

locations.name: The Serene Republic of Venice
locations.description: The capital of banking and naval surveillance. Heart of the Pursuer's network.

locations.name: Scanderoon (Alexandretta)
locations.description: The site of the great duel with the Venetian Galleys. Entry point to the Levant.

locations.name: Milos (Secret Grotto)
locations.description: A quiet obsidian island where Kenelm first performed the Vitriol experiment.

characters.name: Sir Kenelm Digby
characters.bio: The protagonist. Alchemist, courtier, and adventurer.

characters.name: Venetia Stanley
characters.bio: Digby's wife, whose honor he seeks to protect across the Mediterranean.

characters.name: Thomas Allen
characters.bio: Oxford mathematician and mentor. His secret library is the foundation of your knowledge.

timeline.text: They executed him for a dream of gunpowder. Now I must build a dream of salt.
Five men in the dark to protect the Prince? My blade is as certain as my logic.
The channel is a mere gate. The Mediterranean is the laboratory.
An obsidian island. Perfect for the calcination of the copper soul.
They thought a mathematician couldn't command a broadside.

recipes.name: Glass of Antimony
recipes.description: A translucent, ruby-colored glass formed by the intense calcination of Antimony with the aid of Vitriol. The foundation of the Digbyan medical corpus.

recipes.name: Spirit of Vitriol
recipes.description: A sharp, acidic vapor distilled from calcined salts. Essential for the purification of lead.

recipes.name: Philosopher's Lead
recipes.description: Lead purified by the spirit of vitriol, allegedly capable of receiving the lunar influence.

encounters.title: The Battle of Scanderoon
encounters.description: A naval engagement against Venetian and French ships in the Mediterranean.

choices.consequence: Gain immense wealth but risk honor with the Crown.
A more surgical victory, gaining prestige among merchants.

encounters.title: The Mourning Hermit
encounters.description: After Venetia's death, you retreat to a laboratory to master the secrets of matter.

choices.consequence: Knowledge increases but your reputation for credulity grows.
Deep philosophical insight into atomic composites.

encounters.title: Intercepted by the Serene
encounters.description: Your alchemical stench precedes you. A Venetian Galley has overtaken the 'Eagle'. They demand a search of your hold and an explanation for your 'suspicious' experiments at Milos.

choices.consequence: Lose 100 Wealth but maintain your secrets.

choices.text: Defend your right as an English Privateer
choices.consequence: Gain Honor but the Pursuer's Aggro doubles.
